arguments.task: Visit the official Supabase documentation and official first-party pages to verify: 1) one-line description of Supabase, 2) API authentication methods for platform/API usage, including whether OAuth, API keys, tokens, or other auth are documented, 3) pricing/access requirements for API use and whether self-serve or gated, 4) API surface type (REST, GraphQL, or both) and breadth, 5) rate limit notes, 6) whether an official or existing MCP server/integration exists from first-party or official GitHub/MCP registry references, 7) any notable signals like changelog or platform docs. Collect exact supporting URLs from official pages whenever possible. Return concise extracted findings with URLs.

raw_result.description: Open source Firebase alternative for building apps on Postgres with auth, APIs, storage, functions, and realtime features.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs show project API keys for Data APIs (publishable, secret, anon, service_role) and describe JWT-based legacy anon/service_role keys; Management API docs were targeted but not fully captured in the gathered evidence, so platform auth specifics remain partially unverified.
raw_result.gating_reason: Supabase publishes public docs and self-serve pricing with a Free plan, indicating developers can create and use the platform without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs indicate a broad API surface including Data REST API and GraphQL API, alongside platform capabilities such as auth, storage, functions, and management features; exact Management API protocol/auth details were not fully verified from the captured evidence.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Supabase docs include an MCP getting-started page, and official GitHub references were identified for community MCP servers (supabase-mcp and postgres-mcp), but the exact ownership/status should be human-checked.

raw_result.blocker: Some platform/API auth specifics, especially Management API authentication details, were not fully verified from the captured evidence and should be confirmed manually before shipping a complete toolkit.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: A Supabase uptime/rate-limit page was identified as a target, but no limit values were captured from the evidence already gathered; rate-limit specifics need manual verification.
raw_result.notable_signals: Public first-party docs cover API guides, API key models, API security, pricing, and MCP getting started; GitHub repos suggest MCP ecosystem support around Supabase/Postgres.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Management API authentication details, exact rate-limit notes, and the precise official/community status of MCP offerings were not fully verified from the captured page evidence.
